info.title: CA Biositing API
info.description: REST API for CA Biositing bioeconomy data

get.description:
Root endpoint providing API information.

Returns:
    Dictionary with API message and version

Hello endpoint for testing.

Returns:
    Dictionary with hello message

Health check endpoint.

Returns:
    Dictionary with status field set to "ok".

schema.title: Response Health Check V1 Health Get

post.description:
Authenticate with username/password and receive a JWT access token.

Also sets an HTTP-only `access_token` cookie for browser SPA clients.
Create a new API user. Requires admin privileges.

Issue a new access token for the currently authenticated user.

Requires a valid (non-expired) token via Bearer header or cookie.
Also updates the HTTP-only cookie with the new token.

Clear the access_token cookie, effectively logging out browser clients.

Note: the JWT itself remains valid until expiry. Bearer-token clients should
discard the token client-side. Only the HTTP-only cookie is cleared here.

schema.title: Response Logout V1 Auth Logout Post

get.description:
List all distinct USDA crop names available for census queries.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/crops

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of crop name strings

List all distinct resource names available for census queries.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/resources

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of resource name strings

List all distinct geoids available for census queries.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/geoids

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of geoid strings

List all distinct parameter names available for census queries.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/parameters

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of parameter name strings

Get a single census parameter for a specific crop and geographic area.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/crops/corn/geoid/06047/parameters/area harvested

Args:
    session: Database session (injected)
    crop: USDA crop name
    geoid: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code)
    parameter: Parameter name to retrieve

Returns:
    CensusDataResponse with the parameter value and metadata

Raises:
    CropNotFoundException: If crop not found in database
    ParameterNotFoundException: If parameter not found for crop/geoid

parameters.name: crop

schema.description: USDA crop name (e.g., corn, almonds, wheat). Case-insensitive. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/crops to discover available values.
schema.title: Crop

parameters.description: USDA crop name (e.g., corn, almonds, wheat). Case-insensitive. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/crops to discover available values.

parameters.name: geoid

schema.description: Geographic identifier (e.g., 06047, 06077, 06099). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/geoids to discover available values.
schema.title: Geoid

parameters.description: Geographic identifier (e.g., 06047, 06077, 06099). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/geoids to discover available values.

parameters.name: parameter

schema.description: Parameter name (e.g., area harvested, production). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/parameters to discover available values.
schema.title: Parameter

parameters.description: Parameter name (e.g., area harvested, production). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/parameters to discover available values.

get.description:
Get a single census parameter for a specific resource and geographic area.

Resources are mapped to USDA crops internally via the resource mapping table.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/resources/corn stover whole/geoid/06047/parameters/area harvested

Args:
    session: Database session (injected)
    resource: Resource name
    geoid: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code)
    parameter: Parameter name to retrieve

Returns:
    CensusDataResponse with the parameter value and metadata

Raises:
    ResourceNotFoundException: If resource not found in database
    ParameterNotFoundException: If parameter not found for resource/geoid

parameters.name: resource

schema.description: Resource name mapped to a USDA crop (e.g., corn stover whole, almond hulls). Case-insensitive. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/resources to discover available values.
schema.title: Resource

schema.title: Parameter

parameters.description: Parameter name (e.g., area harvested, production). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/parameters to discover available values.

get.description:
List all available census parameters for a specific crop and geographic area.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/usda/census/crops/corn/geoid/06047/parameters

Args:
    session: Database session (injected)
    crop: USDA crop name
    geoid: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code)

Returns:
    CensusListResponse with list of all parameters and their values

Raises:
    CropNotFoundException: If crop not found in database
    ParameterNotFoundException: If no data found for crop/geoid

get.description:
List all distinct resource names available for analysis queries.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of resource name strings

List all distinct geoids available for analysis queries.

Returns an empty list until the known analysis_data_view geoid bug is resolved.
Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources to discover available resources.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/geoids

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of geoid strings

List all distinct parameter names available for analysis queries.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/parameters

Returns:
    DiscoveryResponse with list of parameter name strings

Get a single analysis parameter for a specific resource and geographic area.

Queries analysis data from proximate, ultimate, and compositional analysis records.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources/almond hulls/geoid/06047/parameters/ash

Args:
    session: Database session (injected)
    resource: Resource name
    geoid: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code)
    parameter: Parameter name to retrieve

Returns:
    AnalysisDataResponse with the parameter value and metadata

Raises:
    ResourceNotFoundException: If resource not found in database
    ParameterNotFoundException: If parameter not found for resource/geoid

parameters.name: resource

schema.description: Resource name (e.g., almond hulls, corn stover whole). Case-insensitive; spaces and underscores are treated equivalently. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources to discover available values.
schema.title: Resource

parameters.description: Resource name (e.g., almond hulls, corn stover whole). Case-insensitive; spaces and underscores are treated equivalently. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources to discover available values.

parameters.name: geoid

schema.description: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/geoids to discover available values.
schema.title: Geoid

parameters.description: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/geoids to discover available values.

parameters.name: parameter

schema.description: Parameter name (e.g., ash, moisture, nitrogen). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/parameters to discover available values.
schema.title: Parameter

parameters.description: Parameter name (e.g., ash, moisture, nitrogen). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/parameters to discover available values.

get.description:
List all analysis parameters for a specific resource and geographic area.

Queries all available analysis data from proximate, ultimate, and compositional records.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources/almond hulls/geoid/06047/parameters

Args:
    session: Database session (injected)
    resource: Resource name
    geoid: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code)

Returns:
    AnalysisListResponse with all available parameters and their values

Raises:
    ResourceNotFoundException: If resource not found in database

parameters.name: resource

schema.description: Resource name (e.g., almond hulls, corn stover whole). Case-insensitive; spaces and underscores are treated equivalently. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources to discover available values.
schema.title: Resource

parameters.description: Resource name (e.g., almond hulls, corn stover whole). Case-insensitive; spaces and underscores are treated equivalently. Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/resources to discover available values.

parameters.name: geoid

schema.description: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/geoids to discover available values.
schema.title: Geoid

parameters.description: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code). Use GET /v1/feedstocks/analysis/geoids to discover available values.

get.description:
Get seasonal availability window for a specific resource and geographic area.

Returns the months during which the resource is available for harvest/collection.

Example:
    GET /v1/feedstocks/availability/resources/almond_hulls/geoid/06001

Args:
    session: Database session (injected)
    resource: Resource name
    geoid: Geographic identifier (county FIPS code)

Returns:
    AvailabilityResponse with from_month and to_month values

Raises:
    ResourceNotFoundException: If resource not found or no availability data

parameters.name: resource

schema.description: Resource name (e.g., almond_hulls, corn_stover)
schema.title: Resource

parameters.description: Resource name (e.g., almond_hulls, corn_stover)

parameters.name: geoid

schema.description: Geographic identifier (e.g., 06001)
schema.title: Geoid

parameters.description: Geographic identifier (e.g., 06001)

value.title: Value
value.description: Parameter value (may be null if not available)

AnalysisDataResponse.title: AnalysisDataResponse
AnalysisDataResponse.description:
Response for single feedstock analysis parameter.

Returned when requesting a specific analysis parameter for a resource and geoid.

AnalysisListResponse.title: AnalysisListResponse
AnalysisListResponse.description:
Response for list of feedstock analysis parameters.

Returned when listing all analysis parameters for a resource and geoid.

AvailabilityResponse.title: AvailabilityResponse
AvailabilityResponse.description:
Response for resource availability data.

Returned when requesting seasonal availability for a resource and geoid.

usda_crop.title: Usda Crop
usda_crop.description: USDA crop name (if queried by crop)

value.title: Value
value.description: Parameter value (may be null if not available)

CensusDataResponse.title: CensusDataResponse
CensusDataResponse.description:
Response for single USDA census parameter.

Returned when requesting a specific parameter for a crop/resource and geoid.

usda_crop.title: Usda Crop
usda_crop.description: USDA crop name (if queried by crop)

CensusListResponse.title: CensusListResponse
CensusListResponse.description:
Response for list of USDA census parameters.

Returned when listing all parameters for a crop/resource and geoid.

value.title: Value
value.description: Parameter value (may be null if not available)

DataItemResponse.title: DataItemResponse
DataItemResponse.description:
Individual data item in list responses.

Used when listing all parameters for a crop/resource/geoid combination.
Each item represents one parameter with its value and metadata.

values.title: Values
values.description: Distinct non-NULL values available for querying

DiscoveryResponse.title: DiscoveryResponse
DiscoveryResponse.description: List of distinct queryable values for a given field.

usda_crop.title: Usda Crop
usda_crop.description: USDA crop name (if queried by crop)

value.title: Value
value.description: Parameter value (may be null if not available)

SurveyDataResponse.title: SurveyDataResponse
SurveyDataResponse.description:
Response for single USDA survey parameter.

Similar to CensusDataResponse but for survey data, which includes
additional survey-specific metadata fields.

usda_crop.title: Usda Crop
usda_crop.description: USDA crop name (if queried by crop)

SurveyListResponse.title: SurveyListResponse

Response for list of USDA survey parameters.

Returned when listing all parameters for a crop/resource and geoid.
